Channel 13 reports curbs on Israeli army movements in Lebanon
headlines.snippet: Channel 13 reports that Israel's political leadership has imposed new limits on military operations in southern Lebanon, restricting actions to areas near the 'yellow line' against direct threats and prohibiting operations in places like Beirut and Tyre. This reflects pressure from the Trump administration, with a senior Israeli official stating, 'The message we received in recent weeks from the Americans is clear: You had the right to operate without restrictions, and that's over.'

headlines.title: Former Labour leader revives bill on 'Britain's complicity in genocide'
headlines.snippet: Former Labour leader Jeremy Corbyn has reintroduced a bill demanding an independent public inquiry into what he terms 'Britain's complicity in genocide' in Gaza. He stated, 'Keir Starmer has gone – but we will never forget the role his role his government played in the greatest crime of our time.' The bill seeks an inquiry into UK involvement in Israeli military operations in Gaza, including weapon supply and surveillance aircraft.

headlines.title: Iran says it has no obligation to buy US produce
headlines.snippet: Iran's central bank governor, Abdolnaser Hemmati, stated that Tehran is not obligated to purchase US agricultural products with the released Iranian funds. Speaking to Tasnim news agency, Hemmati clarified that the agreement allows the initial $6 billion to be used for 'basic goods and medicine,' and explicitly said, 'We have no obligation to buy agricultural products from the US.'

headlines.title: Video: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills Palestinian student
headlines.snippet: An Israeli strike in Gaza City's Rimal neighborhood on Monday killed a Palestinian student and wounded three others. Footage from the scene shows civilians initially rushing to the impact site, followed by a scramble for cover during a second strike.
